newsdata.title: NASA Shuts Off Instrument on Voyager 1 to Keep Spacecraft Operating
newsdata.description: On April 17, engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) in Southern California sent commands to shut down an instrument aboard Voyager 1 called the Low-energy Charged Particles experiment, or LECP. The nuclear-powered spacecraft is running low on power, and turning off the LECP is considered the best way to keep humanity’s first interstellar explorer […]

newsdata.title: Vintage NASA: See Voyager’s 1990 ‘Solar System Family Portrait’ Debut
newsdata.description: In archival footage of a historic NASA news conference, the mission reveals history-making images of six planets in our solar system, including a tiny speck called Earth. This week marks 48 years since the Sept. 5, 1977, launch of NASA’s Voyager 1 spacecraft from Cape Canaveral, Florida, to study Jupiter and Saturn up close. Nearly[…]

newsdata.title: NASA’s Voyager 1 Revives Backup Thrusters Before Command Pause
newsdata.description: The mission team wanted to fix the thrusters, deemed unusable decades ago, before the radio antenna that sends commands to the probe went offline for upgrades.

newsdata.title: NASA Turns Off 2 Voyager Science Instruments to Extend Mission
newsdata.description: Mission engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California turned off the cosmic ray subsystem experiment aboard Voyager 1 on Feb. 25 and will shut off Voyager 2’s low-energy charged particle instrument on March 24.

newsdata.title: NASA’s Voyager 1 Resumes Regular Operations After Communications Pause
newsdata.description: Mission engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California turned off the cosmic ray subsystem experiment aboard Voyager 1 on Feb. 25 and will shut off Voyager 2’s low-energy charged particle instrument on March 24.

newsdata.title: NASA Turns Off Science Instrument to Save Voyager 2 Power
newsdata.description: Mission engineers at NASA have turned off the plasma science instrument aboard the Voyager 2 spacecraft due to the probe’s gradually shrinking electrical power supply.

newsdata.title: Voyager 1 will reach one light-day from Earth in 2026
newsdata.description: Voyager 1, NASA’s deep-space probe, could soon become the first spacecraft to reach a historic milestone. In November 2026, the probe will be one light-day from Earth.

newsdata.title: The biggest discoveries of Voyagers — NASA’s most distant explorers
newsdata.description: In 1977, NASA loaded two spacecraft, each the size of a Honda Civic, into a rocket and sent them off on a five-year mission to explore our solar system’s outer planets.

newsdata.title: Voyager 2 enters interstellar space after 41 years
newsdata.description: Mission engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California turned off the cosmic ray subsystem experiment aboard Voyager 1 on Feb. 25 and will shut off Voyager 2’s low-energy charged particle instrument on March 24.
